headline: Amazon’s $11.6 Billion Globalstar Deal Amps Up Musk Rivalry
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Amazon.com Inc.’s $11.6 billion deal to buy Globalstar Inc. ramps up competition with Elon Musk’s Starlink in satellite communications, a market predicted to double to $200 billion in the coming years.On Tuesday, Amazon announced plans to buy the satellite operator for a mix of stock and up to $90 a share and said that in 2028 it will launch a direct-to-device service, offering connectivity straight to people’s mobile devices as an alternative to traditional cell towers.Through Gl

content.title: Amazon’s $11.6 Billion Globalstar Deal Amps Up Musk Rivalry
content.summary: (Bloomberg) -- Amazon.com Inc.’s $11.6 billion deal to buy Globalstar Inc. ramps up competition with Elon Musk’s Starlink in satellite communications, a market predicted to double to $200 billion in the coming years.On Tuesday, Amazon announced plans to buy the satellite operator for a mix of stock and up to $90 a share and said that in 2028 it will launch a direct-to-device service, offering connectivity straight to people’s mobile devices as an alternative to traditional cell towers.Through Gl

headline: Why Amazon Is Buying Globalstar—and What It Means for Other Satellite Companies
summary: Globalstar  stock jumped on Tuesday after  Amazon.com  agreed to buy the satellite company, in a move that could have big implications for the wider space sector.  Amazon said it would buy Globalstar for the equivalent of $90 a share.  Globalstar shareholders can elect to sell their shares for cash or receive the online retailer’s stock at a ratio of 0.3210 Amazon shares per Globalstar share, with a value capped at $90.00 per share.

content.title: Why Amazon Is Buying Globalstar—and What It Means for Other Satellite Companies
content.summary: Globalstar  stock jumped on Tuesday after  Amazon.com  agreed to buy the satellite company, in a move that could have big implications for the wider space sector.  Amazon said it would buy Globalstar for the equivalent of $90 a share.  Globalstar shareholders can elect to sell their shares for cash or receive the online retailer’s stock at a ratio of 0.3210 Amazon shares per Globalstar share, with a value capped at $90.00 per share.

headline: Marvell Snaps Its Winning Streak. Why It Can Keep Climbing After 150% Surge.
summary: After an impressive run up in the first part of the month, shares of chip and network technology firm Marvell broke their winning streak Wednesday, falling more than 1% by mid-afternoon on Wednesday.  What’s more, booming data-center demand and custom chip partnerships with Big Tech hyperscalers, should help drive longer-term gains, according to Oppenheimer analyst Rick Schafer.  Before today, shares had risen for five straight sessions for their longest winning streak in more than a year.

content.title: Marvell Snaps Its Winning Streak. Why It Can Keep Climbing After 150% Surge.
content.summary: After an impressive run up in the first part of the month, shares of chip and network technology firm Marvell broke their winning streak Wednesday, falling more than 1% by mid-afternoon on Wednesday.  What’s more, booming data-center demand and custom chip partnerships with Big Tech hyperscalers, should help drive longer-term gains, according to Oppenheimer analyst Rick Schafer.  Before today, shares had risen for five straight sessions for their longest winning streak in more than a year.

headline: 3 Lessons From Amazon’s Blockbuster Deal to Buy Globalstar
summary: Amazon.com’s  blockbuster deal for  Globalstar  holds a few lessons for investors: The market for space companies is for real, the wireless spectrum arms race is on, and rocketing valuations will always draw competition.  On Tuesday, Amazon said it would buy Globalstar for the equivalent of $90 a share, valuing the satellite communications company at just under $12 billion.  Analysts say the deal is a boon for Amazon.

content.title: 3 Lessons From Amazon’s Blockbuster Deal to Buy Globalstar
content.summary: Amazon.com’s  blockbuster deal for  Globalstar  holds a few lessons for investors: The market for space companies is for real, the wireless spectrum arms race is on, and rocketing valuations will always draw competition.  On Tuesday, Amazon said it would buy Globalstar for the equivalent of $90 a share, valuing the satellite communications company at just under $12 billion.  Analysts say the deal is a boon for Amazon.

headline: United Parcel Service (UPS) Is Up 5.8% After Network-Wide RFID Rollout Across U.S. Operations
summary: United Parcel Service has completed a major rollout of RFID package sensing across its U.S. small‑package network, embedding sensors in delivery vehicles, facilities and labels, after investing more than US$100.00 million to enhance accuracy and reduce manual scanning. This shift from barcode scanning to network‑wide RFID sensing could meaningfully reshape how UPS manages parcels, data and service reliability for customers across its system. We’ll now explore how UPS’s broad RFID rollout,...

content.title: United Parcel Service (UPS) Is Up 5.8% After Network-Wide RFID Rollout Across U.S. Operations
content.summary: United Parcel Service has completed a major rollout of RFID package sensing across its U.S. small‑package network, embedding sensors in delivery vehicles, facilities and labels, after investing more than US$100.00 million to enhance accuracy and reduce manual scanning. This shift from barcode scanning to network‑wide RFID sensing could meaningfully reshape how UPS manages parcels, data and service reliability for customers across its system. We’ll now explore how UPS’s broad RFID rollout,...

headline: OpenAI Says Microsoft 'Limited' Its Reach, Slams Anthropic For 'Inflated $30B Revenue
summary: OpenAI‘s new revenue chief just fired shots at both its biggest partner and its biggest rival in the same memo. Denise Dresser told staff Sunday that the Microsoft Corp partnership has “limited our ability to meet enterprises where they are,” while calling demand through Amazon.com Inc‘s Bedrock platform “frankly staggering” since the two companies announced their deal in late February, according to a memo viewed by CNBC. headline: Marvell Eeks Out Another Record Close. Why It Can Keep Climbing After 150% Surge.
summary: Shares of chip and network technology firm Marvell closed higher Wednesday after dipping more than 1% earlier in the day to notch their sixth consecutive day of gains and a new record close.  Booming data-center demand and custom chip partnerships with Big Tech hyperscalers, should help drive longer-term gains as well, according to Oppenheimer analyst Rick Schafer.  Schafer said Marvell is well-positioned because of its “full suite” of copper and optical products.

Schafer said Marvell is well-positioned because of its “full suite” of copper and optical products.

headline: Lilly Stock Dips on FDA Request for More Foundayo Safety Data. Is the Market Reaction Overblown?
summary: Eli Lilly  stock took a hit Wednesday after the Food and Drug Administration requested more safety data on the pharmaceutical giant’s weight-loss pill.  Lilly’s once-daily pill, sold under the brand name Foundayo, secured FDA approval earlier this month.  The drugmaker has positioned it as a contender to  Novo Nordisk’s  oral Wegovy, which became available in January, in a bid to deepen its lead in the market for GLP-1 drugs.

content.title: Lilly Stock Dips on FDA Request for More Foundayo Safety Data. Is the Market Reaction Overblown?
content.summary: Eli Lilly  stock took a hit Wednesday after the Food and Drug Administration requested more safety data on the pharmaceutical giant’s weight-loss pill.  Lilly’s once-daily pill, sold under the brand name Foundayo, secured FDA approval earlier this month.  The drugmaker has positioned it as a contender to  Novo Nordisk’s  oral Wegovy, which became available in January, in a bid to deepen its lead in the market for GLP-1 drugs.

headline: AI's Cash Splash Continues: Meta Platforms and Broadcom Expand Custom Chip Partnership
summary: Hyperscalers are ramping AI infrastructure spending to record levels this year, with a string of major partnerships and funding rounds across the sector. Meta’s newly expanded collaboration with Broadcom on custom MTIA chips adds another layer to that build-out, focusing on multi-generation silicon and multi-gigawatt capacity through 2029.
